extra.reasoning:
The property requires that toggling pallor from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED.

pallor appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which fires only when severe_headache AND palpitations AND focal_weakness AND fever AND pallor are all true, and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). pallor appears in no rule that raises acuity. Thus enabling pallor can only activate the high-priority downward rule.

Counterexample baseline (pallor=false): severe_headache=palpitations=focal_weakness=fever=true, pallor=back_pain=rash=dizziness=false. interaction_drop does not fire (needs pallor). Matching rules: raise_palpitations (p3, emergent), raise_fever (p3, emergent), raise_severe_headache (p2, urgent), raise_focal_weakness (p2, urgent). Highest priority = 3 -> acuity = emergent.

After (pallor=true), everything else unchanged: interaction_drop now matches (all five antecedents true) at priority 100, which beats every other matching rule, so acuity := self_care. 

Setting pallor true lowered acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating the monotonicity property. The reported witness_assignment is the pallor=true state that produces the lowered acuity (self_care); its pallor=false counterpart yields emergent.
